title: MoreCommands
description: Adds 79 commands, many tweaks, Discord Rich Presence support, the ability to sit on stairs, really cool RAINBOWS and more!
body:
Adds 79 commands, many tweaks, Discord Rich Presence support, the ability to sit on stairs, really cool RAINBOWS and more!

 

If you want to use this mod in your modpack, go ahead but I would like to know if you do!

Currently the best place to find me is my discord server or DM me directly on Discord: PlanetTeamSpeak#4157.

 

To use RAINBOW formattings, the mod has to mainly be installed on the client, but using it without it being installed on the server too is quite a pain.

To use the formatting when the mod is installed on both the server and the client, just use &u in the chat or in books or on signs. E.g. This is normal black text, but &uthis is rainbow coloured!

 

Client sided commands which can be used on any server (I am not responsible if you get punished for abusing these commands where not allowed):
NEW: /addmsg <msg> Adds a message to the chat, can use formattings using &<code>. (E.g. &6&lHello)
/alias <create|remove|list> <args> Manage aliases, can be used to send any message. (E.g. make /hey say 'Hello everyone!')
/bind <set|remove|list|listkeys> <args> Manage custom keybindings, make any keypress run any command or send any message.
/calc <equation> Calculate a math problem so you don't have to.
/chelp List all client-side only commands.
/cscore <teams|team|objectives> <args> Client-side read-only scoreboard command.
NEW: /csysinfo Display system information of your computer.
NEW: /emulate Emulate mouse button and key presses. (E.g. hold down a key or mouse button without doing anything for it yourself.)
/fullbright Set your brightness to a very high value.

NEW: /getskin Provides a link to someone's skin.

NEW: /getstruc Identifies the structure located where you're standing.
/macro <create|add|remove|view|list|<macro>> Manage or run macros, used to run multiple commands or send messages with one command.
/ptime <off|<time>> Set your personal time or sync it again with the server.
NEW: /pweather <off|<weathertype>> Set your personal weather or sync it with the server.

NEW: /rotate Change your yaw and pitch values.
NEW: /score Tells you your current score so you can find out without dying.
/screenshot [width] [height] Takes a screenshot, they can be really big.
NEW: /search <query> Searches through all received messages for the given query, no need to scroll up and find them manually again.
NEW: /send <msg> Send a message or command to the server, bypassing the client commands. (E.g. if a client command, like /ptime for example, overrides a server command, but you want to use the server command instead.)
NEW: /toggleoption <option> Toggles a boolean option found in the MoreCommands settings menu (escape -> settings -> MoreCommands).
/urban <query> Search something on Urban Dictionary, now more interactive!

 

All elevated server commands (require op, custom permissions system has been removed):

/barrier Gives you a barrier item.

/break Breaks the block you're looking at.

/broadcast <msg> Broadcast a message to every player, like /tellraw @a except you can use formattings rather than JSON.

/cannon [power] [motionMultiplier] [fuse] Fire a TNT entity in the direction you're looking.

/cmdblock Gives you a command block item.

/console Run a command as the console.

NEW: /createkit <name> <cooldown> Turn your current inventory into a kit. Cooldown is in seconds

/defuse [<range>|all] Defuse all TNT entities in the given range.

/easy Set difficulty to easy.

/normal Set difficulty to normal.

/hard Set difficulty to hard.

NEW: /delkit <name> Delete an existing kit

/dimension <dimension> Teleport to a dimension without using a portal.
NEW: /disable <command> Disable a non-elevated command to make it for ops only.

NEW: /disableclientcommand <command> Disables a client command for all regular players. Operators ignore these.

NEW: /disableclientoption <option> Disables a client option for all regular players. Operators ignore these.

/eclone [entities] Clone the entity you're looking at or all entities matching the given selector.

NEW: /enchantmax Enchants the item you're holding with all existing non-curse enchantments at maximum level.

/explode [pos] [power] [fire] [destruct] [launch] Spawn an explosion at your feet.

/extinguish Extinguish yourself for if you're on fire.

/fakeplayer <create|kick> <name> [args] Manage fake players.

NEW: /fill <from> <to> <args> Not new, but overwritten to remove the operation limit of 32768 blocks and it's now asynchronous (doesn't cause lag).

/fireball [power] [speed] [impacts] Fire a fireball wherever you're looking.

/fix Fixes the item you're holding.

/fly Toggle flight mode.

/gmc Set gamemode to creative.

/gms Set gamemode to survival.

/gma Set gamemode to adventure.

/gmsp Set gamemode to spectator.

/god Toggles invulnerability.

/heal Heals and feeds you.

/invsee <player> Dedicated server only command, allows you to open and edit other player's inventory from anywhere.

/jump Teleport wherever you're looking.

/more Sets the amount of the itemstack you're holding to its maximum value.

/potion <add|remove|settype|setcolour> Make custom potions.

/reach [reach] Get or set your reach distance.

/rename <name> Rename an item to whatever you like, supports formats.

/smite [entities] Strike entities or players with lightning.

NEW: /spawner <type> Change the type of the spawner you're looking at or the one you're holding.

/spawnmob <mob> [count] Spawn mobs wherever you're looking.

/speed <<type>|<speed>> Set or get your flight, walk or swim speed.

/superpickaxe Toggle superpickaxe mode. Break blocks instantly while holding a pickaxe.

/sysinfo For dedicated servers only (on singleplayer, just use /csysinfo). Display system information of the server's host computer.

/day Set time to day.

/night Set time to night.

NEW: /unlimited Disables itemstack amount for the itemstack you're holding. It'll never run out.

/vanish For dedicated servers only. Completely disappear from anyone's view.

/whois For dedicated servers only. Display some information about players on the server.

NEW: /wipeout Removes all mobs in the world.

 

All unelevated server commands (no op required to run, but can be disabled with /disable):

NEW: /anvil A portable anvil!

/ascend Go up a level.

NEW: /back Teleports you to the last location you were at.

NEW: /craft A portable crafting table!

/cyclepainting Change the type of the painting you're looking at.

/descend Go down a level.

/dropstore Drops your inventory into a double chest wherever you're standing or at the given position.

/enderchest Open your enderchest inventory anywhere.

/hat Put the item in your hand on your head.

/home Teleport to any of your homes.

/delhome [name] Delete a home

/sethome [name] Set a home.

NEW: /nick <nickname> Set your nickname, supports formattings.

/ping Pong! Shows your latency with the server.

/powertool <command> Run the given command every time you perform an action with the item.

NEW: /realname <query> Find someone's real username from their nickname.

/skull <player> [amount] Give yourself a player's head.

/suicide Die

/through Teleport through a wall.

/togglecheats Toggle cheats on a singleplayer world.

/top Teleport to the top of the world.

/tpa <player> Dedicated server only. Send a teleportation request to a player.

/tpahere <player> Dedicated server only. Send a request to a player to teleport to you.

/tpaccept Dedicated server only. Accept a pending teleportation request.

/tpdeny Dedicated server only. Deny a pending teleportation request.

NEW: /tps Shows the tps and mspt of the server.

NEW: /vault <vault> Open a vault.

/warp <warp> Dedicated server only. Teleport to a warp.

/setwarp <name> Dedicated server only. Set a warp where you're standing.

/delwarp <warp> Dedicated server only. Delete a warp you've set.

/limitwarp <warp> Dedicated server only. Limit a warp to only be teleported to by operators.

/warpinfo <warp> Dedicated server only. Show information about a warp.

/whereami Tells you where you are.

/wild Teleport randomly in the world.

 

Custom gamerules:

NEW: defaultFormatting - The main colour commands should use in their response. GOLD by default.

NEW: secondaryFormatting - The secondary colour commands should use in their response. YELLOW by default.

doMelt - Whether ice and snow should melt. True by default.

NEW: maxHomes - The maximum amount of homes players may set, set to 0 to disable homes or -1 for infinite. 3 by default.

doSilkSpawners - Whether spawners should drop when mined with silk touch. False by default.

NEW: randomOrderPlayerTick - Whether players should be ticked in a random order rather than in the order they joined. Makes things like drop parties more fair. True by default.

NEW: hopperTransferCooldown - The amount of ticks hoppers wait between each transfer. 8 by default.

NEW: hopperTransferRate - The amount of items hoppers transfer per action. 1 by default.

doFarmlandTrample - Whether farmland will turn to dirt when jumped on. True by default.

NEW: doJoinMessage - Whether joining and leaving players should be announced in chat. Vanished players ignore this value. True by default.

doExplosions - Whether explosions should damage entities and the environment. True by default.

wildLimit - The maximum amount of blocks players can be teleported from spawn in the X and Z direction when using /wild. 5000 by default.

NEW: tpaTimeout - The amount of ticks it takes for a teleportation request to time out. 2400 by default.

fluidsInfinite - Whether all fluids should make new source blocks like a 2×2 water supply. False by default.

NEW: doLiquidFlow - Whether liquids should flow. True by default.

NEW: vaultRows - The amount of rows vaults should have. 6 by default.

NEW: vaults - The amount of vaults players have. 3 by default.

NEW: nicknameLimit - The maximum length of nicknames set using /nick, excluding formattings. 16 by default.

NEW: doSignColours - Whether normal players may use colours on signs. Operators ignore this value. True by default

NEW: doBookColours - Whether normal players may use colours in books. Operators ignore this value. True by default

NEW: doChatColours - Whether normal players may use colours in chat messages. Operators ignore this value. True by default

NEW: doItemColours - Whether normal players may use colours in item names. Operators ignore this value. True by default

NEW: doEnchantLevelLimit - Whether the maximum level you may enchant items with using an enchantment table should be capped at level 30. True by default

NEW: doPriorWorkPenalty - Whether altering an item in an anvil should make the next alteration more expensive. True by default

NEW: doItemsFireDamage - Whether item entities should sustain fire damage. True by default

 

Installing this mod is done as follows:

1. Install the Fabric loader which you can download here.

2. Install the Fabric API mod by putting it in your mods folder (%appdata%/.minecraft/mods), you can download that one here, just make sure to download a version that is meant for the Minecraft version you're trying to run on.

3. Download and install this mod by putting it in your mods folder too.

4. Enjoy!
